en.extract:
The thick-billed lark (Ramphocoris clotbey) or Clotbey lark is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae.


Taxonomy and systematics
It was named after Antoine Clot. It was originally described as belonging to the genus Melanocorypha and is now placed in the monotypic genus Ramphocoris.


Distribution and habitat
It is found in northern Africa from Mauritania and Morocco to Libya, also in central regions of the Arabian Peninsula. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry shrubland and hot deserts.

The '''thick-billed lark''' ('''''Ramphocoris clotbey''''') or '''Clotbey lark''' is a species of [[lark]] in the family [[Alaudidae]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=9ADB6BA5B67E1B65|title=Ramphocoris clotbey - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-12-09}}</ref>

==Taxonomy and systematics==
It was named after [[Antoine Clot]]. It was originally described as belonging to the genus ''[[Melanocorypha]]'' and is now placed in the [[monotypic]] genus '''''Ramphocoris'''''.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/larks/|title=Nicators, reedling & larks « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|access-date=2016-11-12}}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
It is found in northern Africa from Mauritania and Morocco to Libya, also in central regions of the [[Arabian Peninsula]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical]] or [[tropical]] dry [[shrubland]] and hot [[desert]]s.
